text.title: AI Governance and Compliance Procedure
text.summary: Seven-step procedure from risk classification to signed governance pack.

step.name: classify-risk
step.input: System description; intended use; user impact.
step.action: Apply EU AI Act Annex III to determine class: prohibited / high / limited / minimal. Cross-check with sector-specific rules (medical device, financial, public sector).
step.decision-gate: If prohibited, return refuse-deployment outcome and stop.

step.name: draft-model-card
step.input: Model artefact metadata; training data summary; eval metrics.
step.action: Fill model card template: provenance, intended use, out-of-scope use, metrics, limitations. Pin to model repo @ a commit SHA.

step.name: bias-audit
step.input: Stratified validation set with protected-attribute labels.
step.action: Compute demographic parity difference, equalised odds, disparate impact ratio. Bootstrap 95% CI per metric.

step.name: explainability-artefacts
step.input: Model architecture; tooling (SHAP/LIME or rule trace).
step.action: Generate per-decision sample (≥30), per-feature global importance (or rule trace), per-cohort performance breakdown.

step.name: design-human-oversight
step.action: For each decision class, pick policy: pre-decision-review, post-decision-review with reversal window, sampled-review, none-with-rationale. Name reviewers + SLA.
step.decision-gate: If risk_class=high and any decision class has policy=none, reject design.

step.name: spec-audit-log
step.action: Define structured log entry, endpoint, retention years (≥7 for high), immutability (object-lock or append-only).

step.name: assemble-validate-sign
step.action: Compose pack manifest per content/02-output-contract.xml. Run scripts/validate-output.py. Route to compliance owner. Sign on approval.
step.output: Signed governance pack ready for auditor handoff.
